title: bgc_quast_ppl pipeline parameters
description: BGC-only pipeline: prep, annotation, BGC prediction, and bgc-quast analysis.

input_output_options.title: Input/output options
input_output_options.description: Define where the pipeline should find input data and save output data.

input.description: Path to comma-separated file containing sample names and paths to their FASTA files.
input.help_text: Before running the pipeline, create a samplesheet with information about the genomes to screen. It is a comma-separated file with a header row. The `sample` and `fasta` columns are always required. In compare-to-reference mode, also add a `type` column (`q`/`Q` for query, `r`/`R` for the single reference). See the pipeline README for details and example samplesheets.

outdir.description: The output directory where the results will be saved. You have to use absolute paths to storage on Cloud infrastructure.

email.help_text: Set this parameter to your e-mail address to get a summary e-mail with details of the run sent to you when the workflow exits. If set in your user config file (`~/.nextflow/config`) then you don't need to specify this on the command line for every run.

screening_type_activation.title: Screening type activation
screening_type_activation.description: These parameters influence which workflow (ARG, AMP and/or BGC) to activate.

run_bgc_screening.description: Activate biosynthetic gene cluster screening tools.

annotation_general_options.title: Annotation: general options
annotation_general_options.description: These options influence the generation of annotation files required for downstream steps in ARG, AMP, and BGC workflows.

annotation_tool.description: Specify which annotation tool to use for some downstream tools.

save_annotations.description: Specify whether to save gene annotations in the results directory.

annotation_bakta.title: Annotation: BAKTA
annotation_bakta.description: BAKTA is a tool developed to annotate bacterial genomes and plasmids from both isolates and MAGs. More info: https://github.com/oschwengers/bakta

annotation_bakta_db.description: Specify a path to a local copy of a BAKTA database.
annotation_bakta_db.help_text:
If a local copy of a BAKTA database exists, specify the path to that database which is prepared in a BAKTA format. Otherwise this will be downloaded for you.

The contents of the directory should have files such as `*.dmnd` in the top level.

annotation_bakta_db_downloadtype.description: Download full or light version of the Bakta database if not supplying own database.
annotation_bakta_db_downloadtype.help_text:
If you want the pipeline to download the Bakta database for you, you can choose between the full (33.1 GB) and light (1.3 GB) version. The full version is generally recommended for best annotation results, because it contains all of these:

- UPS: unique protein sequences identified via length and MD5 hash digests (100% coverage & 100% sequence identity)
- IPS: identical protein sequences comprising seeds of UniProt's UniRef100 protein sequence clusters
- PSC: protein sequences clusters comprising seeds of UniProt's UniRef90 protein sequence clusters
- PSCC: protein sequences clusters of clusters comprising annotations of UniProt's UniRef50 protein sequence clusters

If download bandwidth, storage, memory, or run duration requirements become an issue, go for the light version (which only contains PSCCs) by modifying the `annotation_bakta_db_downloadtype` flag.

More details can be found in the [documentation](https://github.com/oschwengers/bakta#database)

>  Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - BAKTA_DBDOWNLOAD: `--type`

annotation_bakta_singlemode.description: Use the default genome-length optimised mode (rather than the metagenome mode).
annotation_bakta_singlemode.help_text:
By default, Bakta's `--meta` mode is used in the pipeline to improve the gene prediction of highly fragmented metagenomes.

By specifying this parameter Bakta will instead use its default mode that is optimised for singular 'complete' genome sequences.

More details can be found in the [documentation](https://github.com/oschwengers/bakta/blob/main/README.md#usage).

>  Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - BAKTA: `--meta`

annotation_bakta_mincontiglen.help_text:
Specify the minimum contig size that would be annotated by BAKTA.
If run with '--annotation_bakta_compliant', the minimum contig length must be set to 200. More details can be found in the [documentation](https://github.com/oschwengers/bakta/blob/main/README.md#usage).

>  Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - BAKTA: `--min-contig-length`

annotation_bakta_translationtable.description: Specify the genetic code translation table.
annotation_bakta_translationtable.help_text:
Specify the genetic code translation table used for translation of nucleotides to amino acids. 
All possible genetic codes (1-25) used for gene annotation can be found [here](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_genetic_codes). More details can be found in the [documentation](https://github.com/oschwengers/bakta/blob/main/README.md#usage).

>  Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - BAKTA: `--translation-table`

annotation_bakta_gram.description: Specify the type of bacteria to be annotated to detect signaling peptides.
annotation_bakta_gram.help_text:
Specify the type of bacteria expected in the input dataset for correct annotation of the signal peptide predictions. More details can be found in the [documentation](https://github.com/oschwengers/bakta/blob/main/README.md#usage).

>  Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - BAKTA: `--gram`

annotation_bakta_complete.description: Specify that all contigs are complete replicons.
annotation_bakta_complete.help_text:
This flag expects contigs that make up complete chromosomes and/or plasmids. By calling it, the user ensures that the contigs are complete replicons. More details can be found in the [documentation](https://github.com/oschwengers/bakta/blob/main/README.md#usage).

>  Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - BAKTA: `--complete`

annotation_bakta_renamecontigheaders.help_text:
This flag specifies that the contig headers should be rewritten. More details can be found in the [documentation](https://github.com/oschwengers/bakta/blob/main/README.md#usage).

>  Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - BAKTA: `--keep-contig-headers`

annotation_bakta_compliant.description: Clean the result annotations to standardise them to Genbank/ENA conventions.
annotation_bakta_compliant.help_text:
The resulting annotations are cleaned up to standardise them to Genbank/ENA/DDJB conventions. CDS without any attributed hits and those without gene symbols or product descriptions different from hypothetical will be marked as 'hypothetical'.
When activated the `--min-contig-length` will be set to 200. More info can be found [here](https://github.com/oschwengers/bakta).

>  Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - BAKTA: `--compliant`

annotation_bakta_trna.help_text:
This flag activates [tRNAscan-SE 2.0](http://lowelab.ucsc.edu/tRNAscan-SE/) that predicts tRNA genes. More details can be found in the [documentation](https://github.com/oschwengers/bakta/blob/main/README.md#usage).

>  Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - BAKTA: `--skip-trna`

annotation_bakta_tmrna.help_text:
This flag activates [Aragorn](http://www.ansikte.se/ARAGORN/) that predicts tmRNA genes. More details can be found in the [documentation](https://github.com/oschwengers/bakta/blob/main/README.md#usage).

>  Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - BAKTA: `--skip-tmrna`
`

annotation_bakta_rrna.help_text:
This flag activates [Infernal vs. Rfam rRNA covariance models](http://eddylab.org/infernal/) that predicts rRNA genes. More details can be found in the [documentation](https://github.com/oschwengers/bakta/blob/main/README.md#usage).

>  Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - BAKTA: `--rrna`

annotation_bakta_ncrna.help_text:
This flag activates [Infernal vs. Rfam ncRNA covariance models](http://eddylab.org/infernal/) that predicts ncRNA genes.
BAKTA distinguishes between ncRNA genes and (cis-regulatory) regions to enable the distinction of feature overlap detection.
This includes distinguishing between ncRNA gene types: sRNA, antisense, ribozyme and antitoxin. More details can be found in the [documentation](https://github.com/oschwengers/bakta/blob/main/README.md#usage).

>  Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - BAKTA: `--ncrna`

annotation_bakta_ncrnaregion.help_text:
This flag activates [Infernal vs. Rfam ncRNA covariance models](http://eddylab.org/infernal/) that predicts ncRNA cis-regulatory regions.
BAKTA distinguishes between ncRNA genes and (cis-regulatory) regions to enable the distinction of feature overlap detection.
This including distinguishing between ncRNA (cis-regulatory) region types: riboswitch, thermoregulator, leader and frameshift element. More details can be found in the [documentation](https://github.com/oschwengers/bakta/blob/main/README.md#usage).

>  Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - BAKTA: `--skip-ncrna-region`

annotation_bakta_crispr.help_text:
This flag activates [PILER-CR](https://www.drive5.com/pilercr/) that predicts CRISPR arrays. More details can be found in the [documentation](https://github.com/oschwengers/bakta/blob/main/README.md#usage).

>  Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - BAKTA: `--skip-crispr`

annotation_bakta_skipcds.help_text:
This flag skips CDS prediction that is done by [PYRODIGAL](https://github.com/althonos/pyrodigal) with which the distinct prediction for complete replicons and uncompleted contigs is done.
For more information on how BAKTA predicts CDS please refer to the BAKTA [documentation](https://github.com/oschwengers/bakta).

>  Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - BAKTA: `--skip-cds`

annotation_bakta_pseudo.help_text:
This flag activates the search for reference Phytochelatin Synthase genes (PCSs) using 'hypothetical' CDS as seed sequences, then aligns the translated PCSs against up-/downstream-elongated CDS regions. More details can be found in the BAKTA [documentation](https://github.com/oschwengers/bakta). 

>  Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - BAKTA: `--skip-pseudo`

annotation_bakta_skipsorf.help_text:
Skip the prediction of sORFs from amino acids stretches as less than 30aa. For more info please refer to BAKTA [documentation](https://github.com/oschwengers/bakta).  All sORF without gene symbols or product descriptions different from hypothetical will be discarded, while only those identified hits exhibiting proper gene symbols or product descriptions different from hypothetical will still be included in the final annotation.

>  Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - BAKTA: `--skip-sorf`

annotation_bakta_gap.help_text:
Activates any gene annotation found within contig assembly gaps. More details can be found in the BAKTA [documentation](https://github.com/oschwengers/bakta). 

>  Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - BAKTA: `--skip-gap`

annotation_bakta_ori.help_text:
Activates the BAKTA search for oriC/oriT genes by comparing results from Blast+ (generated by cov=0.8, id=0.8) and the [MOB-suite](https://github.com/phac-nml/mob-suite) of oriT & [DoriC](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC6323995/) oriC/oriV sequences. Annotations of ori regions take into account overlapping Blast+ hits and are conducted based on a majority vote heuristic. Region edges may be fuzzy. For more info please refer to the BAKTA [documentation](https://github.com/oschwengers/bakta).

>  Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - BAKTA: `--skip-ori`

annotation_bakta_activate_plot.description: Activate generation of circular genome plots.
annotation_bakta_activate_plot.help_text:
Activate this flag to generate genome plots (might be memory-intensive).

>  Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - BAKTA: `--skip-plot`

annotation_bakta_hmms.description: Supply a path of an HMM file of trusted hidden markov models in HMMER format for CDS annotation
annotation_bakta_hmms.help_text:
Bakta accepts user-provided trusted HMMs via `--hmms` in HMMER's text format. If set, Bakta will adhere to the *trusted cutoff* specified in the HMM header. In addition, a max. evalue threshold of 1e-6 is applied. For more info please refer to the BAKTA [documentation](https://github.com/oschwengers/bakta).

>  Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - BAKTA: `--hmms`

annotation_prokka.title: Annotation: Prokka
annotation_prokka.description: Prokka annotates genomic sequences belonging to bacterial, archaeal and viral genomes. More info: https://github.com/tseemann/prokka

annotation_prokka_singlemode.description: Use the default genome-length optimised mode (rather than the metagenome mode).
annotation_prokka_singlemode.help_text:
By default, Prokka's `--metagenome` mode is used in the pipeline to improve the gene prediction of highly fragmented metagenomes.

By specifying this parameter Prokka will instead use its default mode that is optimised for singular 'complete' genome sequences.

For more information, please check the Prokka [documentation](https://github.com/tseemann/prokka).

> Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - Prokka: `--metagenome`

annotation_prokka_rawproduct.description: Suppress the default clean-up of the gene annotations.
annotation_prokka_rawproduct.help_text:
By default, annotation in Prokka is carried out by alignment to other proteins in its database, or the databases the user provides via the tools `--proteins` flag. The resulting annotations are then cleaned up to standardise them to Genbank/ENA conventions.
'Vague names' are set to 'hypothetical proteins', 'possible/probable/predicted' are set to 'putative' and 'EC/CPG and locus tag ids' are removed.

By supplying this flag you stop such clean up leaving the original annotation names.

For more information please check the Prokka [documentation](https://github.com/tseemann/prokka).

This flag suppresses this default behavior of Prokka (which is to perform the cleaning).

> Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - Prokka: `--rawproduct`

annotation_prokka_kingdom.description: Specify the kingdom that the input represents.
annotation_prokka_kingdom.help_text:
Specifies the kingdom that the input sample is derived from and/or you wish to screen for

> ⚠️ Prokka cannot annotate Eukaryotes.

For more information please check the Prokka [documentation](https://github.com/tseemann/prokka).

> Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - Prokka: `--kingdom`

annotation_prokka_gcode.description: Specify the translation table used to annotate the sequences.
annotation_prokka_gcode.help_text:
Specify the translation table used to annotate the sequences. All possible genetic codes (1-25) used for gene annotation can be found [here](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_genetic_codes). This flag is required if the flag `--kingdom` is assigned.

For more information please check the Prokka [documentation](https://github.com/tseemann/prokka).

> Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - Prokka: `--gcode`

annotation_prokka_mincontiglen.description: Minimum contig size required for annotation (bp).
annotation_prokka_mincontiglen.help_text:
Specify the minimum contig lengths to carry out annotations on. The Prokka developers recommend that this should be ≥ 200 bp, if you plan to submit such annotations to NCBI.

For more information please check the Prokka [documentation](https://github.com/tseemann/prokka).

> Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - Prokka: `--mincontiglen`

annotation_prokka_evalue.help_text:
Specifiy the maximum E-value used for filtering the alignment hits.

For more information please check the Prokka [documentation](https://github.com/tseemann/prokka).

> Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - Prokka: `--evalue`

annotation_prokka_coverage.help_text:
Specify the minimum coverage percent of the annotated genome. This must be set between 0-100.

For more information please check the Prokka [documentation](https://github.com/tseemann/prokka).

> Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - Prokka: `--coverage`

annotation_prokka_cdsrnaolap.description: Allow transfer RNA (trRNA) to overlap coding sequences (CDS).
annotation_prokka_cdsrnaolap.help_text:
Allow transfer RNA (trRNA) to overlap coding sequences (CDS). Transfer RNAs are short stretches of nucleotide sequences that link mRNA and the amino acid sequence of proteins. Their presence helps in the annotation of the sequences, because each trRNA can only be attached to one type of amino acid.

For more information please check the Prokka [documentation](https://github.com/tseemann/prokka).

> Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - Prokka: `--cdsrnaolap`

annotation_prokka_rnammer.help_text:
Activates [RNAmmer](https://services.healthtech.dtu.dk/service.php?RNAmmer-1.2) instead of the Prokka default [Barrnap](https://github.com/tseemann/barrnap) for rRNA prediction during the annotation process. RNAmmer classifies ribosomal RNA genes in genome sequences by using two levels of Hidden Markov Models. Barrnap uses the nhmmer tool that includes HMMER 3.1 for HMM searching in RNA:DNA style.

For more information please check the Prokka [documentation](https://github.com/tseemann/prokka).

> Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - Prokka: `--rnammer`

annotation_prokka_compliant.description: Force contig name to Genbank/ENA/DDJB naming rules.
annotation_prokka_compliant.help_text:
Force the contig headers to conform to the Genbank/ENA/DDJB contig header standards. This is activated in combination with `--centre [X]` when contig headers supplied by the user are non-conforming and therefore need to be renamed before Prokka can start annotation. This flag activates  `--genes --mincontiglen 200`. For more information please check the Prokka [documentation](https://github.com/tseemann/prokka). 

> Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - Prokka: `--compliant`

annotation_prokka_addgenes.description: Add the gene features for each CDS hit.
annotation_prokka_addgenes.help_text:
For every CDS annotated, this flag adds the gene that encodes for that CDS region. For more information please check the Prokka [documentation](https://github.com/tseemann/prokka). 

> Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - Prokka: `--addgenes`

annotation_prokka_retaincontigheaders.help_text:
This parameter allows prokka to retain the original contig names by activating `PROKKA`'s  `--force` flag. If this parameter is set to `false` it activates `PROKKA`'s flags `--locus-tag PROKKA --centre CENTER` so the locus tags (contig names) will be PROKKA_# and the center tag will be CENTER. By default `PROKKA` changes contig headers to avoid errors that might rise due to long contig headers, so this must be turned on if the user has short contig names that should be retained by `PROKKA`. 

> Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - Prokka: `--locus-tag PROKKA --centre CENTER`
> - Prokka: `--force`

annotation_prodigal.title: Annotation: Prodigal
annotation_prodigal.description: Prodigal is a protein-coding gene prediction tool developed to run on bacterial and archaeal genomes. More info: https://github.com/hyattpd/prodigal/wiki

annotation_prodigal_singlemode.description: Specify whether to use Prodigal's single-genome mode for long sequences.
annotation_prodigal_singlemode.help_text:
By default Prodigal runs in 'single genome' mode that requires sequence lengths to be equal or longer than 20000 characters.

However, more fragmented reads from MAGs often result in contigs shorter than this. Therefore, this pipeline will run with the `meta` mode by default. Providing this parameter allows to override this and run in single genome mode again.

For more information check the Prodigal [documentation](https://github.com/hyattpd/prodigal/wiki).

> Modifies tool parameter(s): 
> -PRODIGAL: `-p`

annotation_prodigal_closed.description: Does not allow partial genes on contig edges.
annotation_prodigal_closed.help_text:
Suppresses partial genes from being on contig edge, resulting in closed ends. Should only be activated for genomes where it is sure the first and last bases of the sequence(s) do not fall inside a gene. Run together with `-p normal` (former `-p single`) .

For more information check the Prodigal [documentation](https://github.com/hyattpd/prodigal/wiki).

> Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - PRODIGAL: `-c`

annotation_prodigal_transtable.description: Specifies the translation table used for gene annotation.
annotation_prodigal_transtable.help_text:
Specifies which translation table should be used for seqeunce annotation. All possible genetic code translation tables can be found [here](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_genetic_codes). The default is set at 11, which is used for standard Bacteria/Archeae.

For more information check the Prodigal [documentation](https://github.com/hyattpd/prodigal/wiki).

> Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - PRODIGAL: `-g`

annotation_prodigal_forcenonsd.description: Forces Prodigal to scan for motifs.
annotation_prodigal_forcenonsd.help_text:
Forces PRODIGAL to a full scan for motifs rather than activating the Shine-Dalgarno RBS finder, the default scanner for PRODIGAL to train for motifs.

For more information check the Prodigal [documentation](https://github.com/hyattpd/prodigal/wiki).

> Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - PRODIGAL: `-n`

annotation_pyrodigal.title: Annotation: Pyrodigal
annotation_pyrodigal.description: Pyrodigal is a resource-optimized wrapper around Prodigal, producing protein-coding gene predictions of bacterial and archaeal genomes. Read more at the Pyrodigal GitHub repository (https://github.com/althonos/pyrodigal) or its documentation (https://pyrodigal.readthedocs.io).

annotation_pyrodigal_singlemode.description: Specify whether to use Pyrodigal's single-genome mode for long sequences.
annotation_pyrodigal_singlemode.help_text:
By default Pyrodigal runs in 'single genome' mode that requires sequence lengths to be equal or longer than 20000 characters.

However, more fragmented reads from MAGs often result in contigs shorter than this. Therefore, this pipeline will run with the `meta` mode by default, but providing this parameter allows to override this and run in single genome mode again.

For more information check the Pyrodigal [documentation](https://pyrodigal.readthedocs.io).

> Modifies tool parameter(s): 
> - PYRODIGAL: `-p`

annotation_pyrodigal_closed.description: Does not allow partial genes on contig edges.
annotation_pyrodigal_closed.help_text:
Suppresses partial genes from being on contig edge, resulting in closed ends. Should only be activated for genomes where it is sure the first and last bases of the sequence(s) do not fall inside a gene. Run together with `-p single` .

For more information check the Pyrodigal [documentation](https://pyrodigal.readthedocs.io).

> Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - PYRODIGAL: `-c`

annotation_pyrodigal_transtable.description: Specifies the translation table used for gene annotation.
annotation_pyrodigal_transtable.help_text:
Specifies which translation table should be used for seqeunce annotation. All possible genetic code translation tables can be found [here](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_genetic_codes). The default is set at 11, which is used for standard Bacteria/Archeae.

For more information check the Pyrodigal [documentation](https://pyrodigal.readthedocs.io).

> Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - PYRODIGAL: `-g`

annotation_pyrodigal_forcenonsd.description: Forces Pyrodigal to scan for motifs.
annotation_pyrodigal_forcenonsd.help_text:
Forces Pyrodigal to a full scan for motifs rather than activating the Shine-Dalgarno RBS finder, the default scanner for Pyrodigal to train for motifs.

For more information check the Pyrodigal [documentation](https://pyrodigal.readthedocs.io).

> Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - PYRODIGAL: `-n`

annotation_pyrodigal_usespecialstopchara.description: This forces Pyrodigal to append asterisks (`*`) as stop codon indicators. Do not use when running AMP workflow.
annotation_pyrodigal_usespecialstopchara.help_text:
Some downstream tools like AMPlify cannot process sequences containing non-sequence characters like the stop codon indicator `*`. Thus, this flag is deactivated by default. Activate this flag to revert the behaviour and have Pyrodigal append `*` as stop codon indicator to annotated sequences.

For more information check the Pyrodigal [documentation](https://pyrodigal.readthedocs.io).

> Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - PYRODIGAL: `--no-stop-codon`

save_db.description: Specify whether to save pipeline-downloaded databases in your results directory.
save_db.help_text:
This pipeline can download databases for you, but these are often very large and can significantly slow down runtime if downloaded every run.

Specifying `--save_db` will save the pipeline-downloaded databases in your results directory. This applies to: antiSMASH and DeepBGC.

You can then move the resulting directories/files to a central cache directory of your choice for re-use in the future.

If you do not specify this flag, the database files will remain in your `work/` directory and will be deleted if `cleanup = true` is specified in your config, or if you run `nextflow clean`.

bgc_general_options.title: BGC: general options
bgc_general_options.description: These parameters influence general BGC settings like minimum input sequence length.

bgc_mincontiglength.description: Specify the minimum length of contigs that go into BGC screening.
bgc_mincontiglength.help_text:
Specify the minimum length of contigs that go into BGC screening.

If BGC screening is turned on, this pipeline will generate for each input sample a second FASTA file of only contigs that are longer than the specified minimum length.
This is due to an (approximate) 'biological' minimum length that nucleotide sequences would need to have to code for a valid BGC (e.g. not on the edge of a contig), as well as to speeding up BGC screening by screening only meaningful contigs.

bgc_savefilteredcontigs.description: Specify to save the length-filtered (unannotated) FASTAs used for BGC screening.

bgc_antismash.title: BGC: antiSMASH
bgc_antismash.description: Biosynthetic gene cluster detection. More info: https://docs.antismash.secondarymetabolites.org

bgc_skip_antismash.description: Skip antiSMASH during the BGC screening.

bgc_antismash_db.description: Path to user-defined local antiSMASH database.
bgc_antismash_db.help_text:
It is recommend to pre-download the antiSMASH databases to your machine and pass the path of it to this parameter, as this can take a long time to download - particularly when running lots of pipeline runs.

The contents of the database directory should include directories such as `as-js/`, `clusterblast/`, `clustercompare/` etc. in the top level. This must be an antiSMASH v8 database.

See the pipeline [README](https://github.com/Seg0fieD/bgc_quast_ppl) for details on how to download this.

bgc_antismash_contigminlength.description: Minimum length a contig must have to be screened with antiSMASH.
bgc_antismash_contigminlength.help_text:
This specifies the minimum length that a contig must have for the contig to be screened by antiSMASH.

For more information see the antiSMASH [documentation](https://docs.antismash.secondarymetabolites.org/).

This will only apply to samples that are screened with antiSMASH (i.e., those samples that have not been removed by `--bgc_antismash_sampleminlength`).

You may wish to increase this value compared to that of `--bgc_antismash_sampleminlength`, in cases where you wish to screen higher-quality (i.e. longer) contigs, or speed up runs by not screening lower quality/less informative contigs.

> Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - antiSMASH: `--minlength`

bgc_antismash_cbgeneral.description: Turn on clusterblast comparison against database of antiSMASH-predicted clusters.
bgc_antismash_cbgeneral.help_text:
Compare identified clusters against a database of antiSMASH-predicted clusters using the clusterblast algorithm.

For more information see the antiSMASH [documentation](https://docs.antismash.secondarymetabolites.org/).

> Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - antiSMASH: `--cb-general`

bgc_antismash_cbknownclusters.description: Turn on clusterblast comparison against known gene clusters from the MIBiG database.
bgc_antismash_cbknownclusters.help_text:
This will turn on comparing identified clusters against known gene clusters from the MIBiG database using the clusterblast algorithm.

[MIBiG](https://mibig.secondarymetabolites.org/) is a curated database of experimentally characterised gene clusters and with rich associated metadata.

For more information see the antiSMASH [documentation](https://docs.antismash.secondarymetabolites.org/).

> Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - antiSMASH: `--cb-knownclusters`

bgc_antismash_cbsubclusters.description: Turn on clusterblast comparison against known subclusters responsible for synthesising precursors.
bgc_antismash_cbsubclusters.help_text:
Turn on additional screening for operons involved in the biosynthesis of early secondary metabolites components using the clusterblast algorithm.

For more information see the antiSMASH [documentation](https://docs.antismash.secondarymetabolites.org/).

> Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - antiSMASH: `--cb-subclusters`

bgc_antismash_ccmibig.description: Turn on ClusterCompare comparison against known gene clusters from the MIBiG database.
bgc_antismash_ccmibig.help_text:
Turn on comparison of detected genes against the MIBiG database using the ClusterCompare algorithm - an alternative to clusterblast.

Note there will not be a dedicated ClusterCompare output in the antiSMASH results directory, but is present in the HTML.

For more information see the antiSMASH [documentation](https://docs.antismash.secondarymetabolites.org/).

> Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - antiSMASH: `--cc-mibig`

bgc_antismash_smcogtrees.description: Generate phylogenetic trees of secondary metabolite group orthologs.
bgc_antismash_smcogtrees.help_text:
Turning this on will activate the generation of additional functional and phylogenetic analysis of genes, via comparison against databases of protein orthologs.

For more information see the antiSMASH [documentation](https://docs.antismash.secondarymetabolites.org/).

> Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - antiSMASH: `--cb-smcog-trees`

bgc_antismash_hmmdetectionstrictness.description: Defines which level of strictness to use for HMM-based cluster detection.
bgc_antismash_hmmdetectionstrictness.help_text:
Levels of strictness correspond to screening different groups of 'how well-defined' clusters are. For example, `loose` will include screening for 'poorly defined' clusters (e.g. saccharides), `relaxed` for partially present clusters (e.g. certain types of NRPS), whereas `strict` will screen for well-defined clusters such as Ketosynthases.

You can see the rules for the levels of strictness [here](https://github.com/antismash/antismash/tree/master/antismash/detection/hmm_detection/cluster_rules).

For more information see the antiSMASH [documentation](https://docs.antismash.secondarymetabolites.org/).

> Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - antiSMASH: `--hmmdetection-strictness`

bgc_antismash_pfam2go.description: Run Pfam to Gene Ontology mapping module.
bgc_antismash_pfam2go.help_text:
This maps the proteins to Pfam database to annotate BGC modules with functional information based on the protein families they contain. For more information see the antiSMASH [documentation](https://docs.antismash.secondarymetabolites.org/).

> Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - antiSMASH: `--pfam2go`

bgc_antismash_rre.description: Run RREFinder precision mode on all RiPP gene clusters.
bgc_antismash_rre.help_text:
This enables the prediction of regulatory elements on the BGC that help in the control of protein expression. For more information see the antiSMASH [documentation](https://docs.antismash.secondarymetabolites.org/).

> Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - antiSMASH: `--rre`

bgc_antismash_taxon.description: Specify which taxonomic classification of input sequence to use.
bgc_antismash_taxon.help_text:
This specifies which set of secondary metabolites to screen for, based on the taxon type the secondary metabolites are from.

This will run different pipelines depending on whether the input sequences are from bacteria or fungi.

For more information see the antiSMASH [documentation](https://docs.antismash.secondarymetabolites.org/).

> Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - antiSMASH: `--taxon`

bgc_antismash_tfbs.description: Run TFBS finder on all gene clusters.
bgc_antismash_tfbs.help_text:
This enables the prediction of transcription factor binding sites which control the gene expression. For more information see the antiSMASH [documentation](https://docs.antismash.secondarymetabolites.org/).

> Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - antiSMASH: `--tfbs`

bgc_antismash_minimal.description: Run antiSMASH in minimal mode (skip extra analyses; faster). Default. Does not use a smaller database.
bgc_antismash_minimal.help_text:
Turns on antiSMASH --minimal, which only runs core BGC detection and skips the extra analysis modules. This is the default. Controls analysis depth, not database size.

> Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - antiSMASH: `--minimal`

bgc_antismash_full.description: Run antiSMASH full analysis instead of the default minimal mode.
bgc_antismash_full.help_text:
Turns OFF antiSMASH --minimal so all extra analysis modules run. Minimal is the default; set this flag only when you want the full analysis. Cannot be combined with --bgc_antismash_minimal.

> Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - antiSMASH: (omits `--minimal`)

bgc_deepbgc.title: BGC: DeepBGC
bgc_deepbgc.description: A deep learning genome-mining strategy for biosynthetic gene cluster prediction. More info: https://github.com/Merck/deepbgc/tree/master/deepbgc

bgc_skip_deepbgc.description: Skip DeepBGC during the BGC screening.

bgc_deepbgc_db.description: Path to local DeepBGC database folder.
bgc_deepbgc_db.help_text:
The contents of the database directory should include directories such as `common`, `0.1.0` in the top level.

For more information see the DeepBGC [documentation](https://github.com/Merck/deepbgc).

> Modifies tool parameter(s)
> -  DeepBGC: environment variable `DEEPBGC_DOWNLOADS_DIR`

bgc_deepbgc_score.description: Average protein-wise DeepBGC score threshold for extracting BGC regions from Pfam sequences.
bgc_deepbgc_score.help_text:
The DeepBGC score threshold for extracting BGC regions from Pfam sequences based on average protein-wise value.

For more information see the DeepBGC [documentation](https://github.com/Merck/deepbgc).

> Modifies tool parameter(s)
> -  DeepBGC: `--score`

bgc_deepbgc_prodigalsinglemode.description: Run DeepBGC's internal Prodigal step in `single` mode to restrict detecting genes to long contigs
bgc_deepbgc_prodigalsinglemode.help_text:
By default DeepBGC's Prodigal runs in 'single genome' mode that requires sequence lengths to be equal or longer than 20000 characters.

However, more fragmented reads from MAGs often result in contigs shorter than this. Therefore, this pipeline will run with the `meta` mode by default, but providing this parameter allows to override this and run in single genome mode again.

For more information check the Prodigal [documentation](https://github.com/hyattpd/prodigal/wiki).

> Modifies tool parameter(s)
> -  DeepBGC: `--prodigal-meta-mode`

bgc_deepbgc_mergemaxproteingap.description: Merge detected BGCs within given number of proteins.
bgc_deepbgc_mergemaxproteingap.help_text:
Merge detected BGCs within given number of proteins.

For more information see the DeepBGC [documentation](https://github.com/Merck/deepbgc).

> Modifies tool parameter(s)
> -  DeepBGC: `--merge-max-protein-gap`

bgc_deepbgc_mergemaxnuclgap.description: Merge detected BGCs within given number of nucleotides.
bgc_deepbgc_mergemaxnuclgap.help_text:
Merge detected BGCs within given number of nucleotides.

For more information see the DeepBGC [documentation](https://github.com/Merck/deepbgc).

> Modifies tool parameter(s)
> - DeepBGC: `--merge-max-nucl-gap`

bgc_deepbgc_minnucl.help_text:
Minimum length a BGC must have (in bp) to be reported as detected.

For more information see the DeepBGC [documentation](https://github.com/Merck/deepbgc).

> Modifies tool parameter(s)
> -  DeepBGC: `--min-nucl`

bgc_deepbgc_minproteins.description: Minimum number of proteins in a BGC.
bgc_deepbgc_minproteins.help_text:
Minimum number of proteins in a BGC must have to be reported as 'detected'.

For more information see the DeepBGC [documentation](https://github.com/Merck/deepbgc).

> Modifies tool parameter(s)
> -  DeepBGC: `--min-proteins`

bgc_deepbgc_mindomains.description: Minimum number of protein domains in a BGC.
bgc_deepbgc_mindomains.help_text:
Minimum number of domains a BGC must have to be reported as 'detected'.

For more information see the DeepBGC [documentation](https://github.com/Merck/deepbgc).

> Modifies tool parameter(s)
> -  DeepBGC: `--min-domains`

bgc_deepbgc_minbiodomains.description: Minimum number of known biosynthetic (as defined by antiSMASH) protein domains in a BGC.
bgc_deepbgc_minbiodomains.help_text:
Minimum number of biosynthetic protein domains a BGC must have to be reported as 'detected'. This is based on antiSMASH definitions.

For more information see the DeepBGC [documentation](https://github.com/Merck/deepbgc).

> Modifies tool parameter(s)
> -  DeepBGC: `--min-bio-domains`

bgc_deepbgc_classifierscore.description: DeepBGC classification score threshold for assigning classes to BGCs.
bgc_deepbgc_classifierscore.help_text:
DeepBGC classification score threshold for assigning classes to BGCs.

For more information see the DeepBGC [documentation](https://github.com/Merck/deepbgc).

> Modifies tool parameter(s)
> -  DeepBGC: `--classifier-score`

bgc_gecco.title: BGC: GECCO
bgc_gecco.description: Biosynthetic gene cluster detection using Conditional Random Fields (CRFs). More info: https://gecco.embl.de

bgc_skip_gecco.description: Skip GECCO during the BGC screening.

bgc_gecco_mask.description: Enable unknown region masking to prevent genes from stretching across unknown nucleotides.
bgc_gecco_mask.help_text:
Enable unknown region masking to prevent genes from stretching across unknown nucleotides during ORF detection based on P(y)rodigal.

For more information see the GECCO [documentation](https://github.com/zellerlab/GECCO).

> Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - GECCO: `--mask`

bgc_gecco_cds.description: The minimum number of coding sequences a valid cluster must contain.
bgc_gecco_cds.help_text:
Specify the number of consecutive genes a hit must have to be considered as part of a possible BGC region during BGC extraction.

For more information see the GECCO [documentation](https://github.com/zellerlab/GECCO).

> Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - GECCO: `--cds`

bgc_gecco_pfilter.description: The p-value cutoff for protein domains to be included.
bgc_gecco_pfilter.help_text:
For more information see the GECCO [documentation](https://github.com/zellerlab/GECCO).

> Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - GECCO: `--pfilter`

bgc_gecco_threshold.description: The probability threshold for cluster detection.
bgc_gecco_threshold.help_text:
Specify the minimum probability a predicted gene must have to be considered as part of a BGC during BGC extraction.

Reducing this value may increase number and length of hits, but will reduce the accuracy of the predictions.

For more information see the GECCO [documentation](https://github.com/zellerlab/GECCO).

> Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - GECCO: `--threshold`

bgc_gecco_edgedistance.description: The minimum number of annotated genes that must separate a cluster from the edge.
bgc_gecco_edgedistance.help_text:
The minimum number of annotated genes that must separate a possible BGC cluster from the edge. Edge clusters will still be included if they are longer. A lower number will increase the number of false positives on small contigs. Used during BGC extraction.

For more information see the GECCO [documentation](https://github.com/zellerlab/GECCO).

> Modifies tool parameter(s):
> - GECCO: `--edge-distance`

institutional_config_options.title: Institutional config options
institutional_config_options.description: Parameters used to describe centralised config profiles. These should not be edited.
institutional_config_options.help_text: The centralised nf-core configuration profiles use a handful of pipeline parameters to describe themselves. This information is then printed to the Nextflow log when you run a pipeline. You should not need to change these values when you run a pipeline.

custom_config_version.description: Git commit id for Institutional configs.

custom_config_base.help_text: If you're running offline, Nextflow will not be able to fetch the institutional config files from the internet. If you don't need them, then this is not a problem. If you do need them, you should download the files from the repo and tell Nextflow where to find them with this parameter.

generic_options.title: Generic options
generic_options.description: Less common options for the pipeline, typically set in a config file.
generic_options.help_text:
These options are common to all nf-core pipelines and allow you to customise some of the core preferences for how the pipeline runs.

Typically these options would be set in a Nextflow config file loaded for all pipeline runs, such as `~/.nextflow/config`.

publish_dir_mode.description: Method used to save pipeline results to output directory.
publish_dir_mode.help_text: The Nextflow `publishDir` option specifies which intermediate files should be saved to the output directory. This option tells the pipeline what method should be used to move these files. See [Nextflow docs](https://www.nextflow.io/docs/latest/process.html#publishdir) for details.

email_on_fail.description: Email address for completion summary, only when pipeline fails.
email_on_fail.help_text: An email address to send a summary email to when the pipeline is completed - ONLY sent if the pipeline does not exit successfully.

plaintext_email.description: Send plain-text email instead of HTML.

monochrome_logs.description: Do not use coloured log outputs.

hook_url.description: Incoming hook URL for messaging service
hook_url.help_text: Incoming hook URL for messaging service. Currently, MS Teams and Slack are supported.

validate_params.description: Boolean whether to validate parameters against the schema at runtime

pipelines_testdata_base_path.description: Base URL or local path to location of pipeline test dataset files

trace_report_suffix.description: Suffix to add to the trace report filename. Default is the date and time in the format yyyy-MM-dd_HH-mm-ss.

max_resource_options.title: Max resource options
max_resource_options.description: Set the top-end limit for compute resources used by any single job.
max_resource_options.help_text: Each job requests resources by label. These three values cap those requests so jobs never ask for more than your machine has. Raise them if you have a bigger machine.

max_cpus.description: Maximum number of CPUs any single job may use.

max_memory.description: Maximum memory any single job may use. Example: '8.GB'.

max_time.description: Maximum time any single job may run. Example: '24.h'.

bgc_quast.title: BGC-QUAST: MODE
bgc_quast.description: Options for the final bgc-quast analysis step. The QUAST output directory and BGC prediction paths are wired in code; only override them if needed.

bgc_quast_mode.description: Which bgc-quast comparison mode to run.
bgc_quast_mode.help_text: Passed to bgc-quast --mode. compare-samples: one report per tool across samples. compare-tools: per-sample, tools joined. compare-to-reference: each query vs one shared reference (turns on QUAST + reference lane). auto: placeholder, errors on purpose.

bgc_quast_quastdir.description: Optional path to a pre-made QUAST output directory. Only needed to override the directory the pipeline produces.

bgc_quast_edge_distance.description: Distance (bp) from a contig edge used to label a BGC as complete or partial.
bgc_quast_edge_distance.help_text: Passed to bgc-quast --edge-distance. This is a labelling threshold for completeness, not a contig filter.

bgc_quast_min_bgc_length.description: Minimum BGC length (bp) to keep. 0 means no minimum.

bgc_quast_merge_distance.description: Merge nearby BGCs if the gap between them is at or below this distance (bp). 0 turns merging off.

bgc_quast_overlap_fraction.description: BGC overlap threshold as a fraction between 0 and 1. Used in compare-tools mode.

bgc_quast_output_bgcs.description: Write BGCs predicted by all tools into one combined GenBank file. Used in compare-tools mode and needs a genome.

bgc_quast_debug.description: Keep bgc-quast intermediate files for debugging.
